title: T-028 — API + worker: D365 optional import/export sync job with idempotency
prompt:
# T-028 — API + worker: D365 optional import/export sync job with idempotency

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §13.1, §13.3, §13.5, §13.10
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Optional D365 integration worker with idempotency, diff/audit, and authorized overwrite-only semantics.

Use msw or nock to mock D365 endpoints in tests. Cron registration in lib/cron/registry.ts. Connection config from 02-SETTINGS §11.3 (encrypted via pgcrypto). Failure path writes to d365_sync_dlq. D365 is integration only; Monopilot-owned data is canonical. Pull/import may stage diffs but must not silently overwrite local product/BOM/WIP data.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/d365/sync/route.ts [create]
- lib/integrations/d365/pull.ts [create]
- lib/integrations/d365/idempotency.ts [create]
- lib/cron/d365-pull.ts [create]
- tests/wiring/d365-pull.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Push (T-029)
- DLQ UI (T-058)
- Drift resolution UI (T-059)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given integration.d365.enabled=false, when POST /sync runs, then 412 V-TEC-70.
2. Given local Monopilot data differs from incoming D365 data, when worker processes record, then audit row 'd365_drift' or an import-diff row is written and canonical Monopilot rows are not overwritten without authorized workflow.
3. Given duplicate idempotency_key, when worker runs again, then second attempt is no-op.
4. Given mocked D365 returns 3 items modified, when pull completes, then d365_sync_jobs.records_processed=3.

## Test strategy
- RED tests using msw/nock for V-TEC-70..73 paths.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/d365-pull.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not call live D365 in tests.
- Do not store credentials in plaintext.
- Do not make D365 the canonical source of Technical master data; no silent overwrite/import from D365.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
